copy.title: Spurs force Game 7. Microsoft soars three percent. Charles Schwab lead holds.
copy.keyTakeaway: Spurs force Game 7 with dominant 118-91 win to stay alive in West Finals. Tech stocks pop on broad gains led by Microsoft's 3.5% jump. Box office surprise as Obsession spikes in second weekend.

todaysHits.sports: Spurs dominate Game 6, force deciding Game 7 against Thunder.
todaysHits.golf: Ryan Gerard leads Charles Schwab Challenge at 6-under.
todaysHits.culture: Obsession pulls off rare box office second-weekend spike.

lead.headline: Spurs force Game 7 with dominant road shutout
lead.whatHappened: San Antonio dismantled Oklahoma City 118-91 in Game 6, evening the Western Conference Finals at 3-3 and sending the series to a decider. The Spurs' defense was suffocating — they completely shut down the Thunder's offense when it mattered most.
lead.whyBullet1: A Game 7 in the Finals is basically a coin flip, and San Antonio just grabbed it by the throat instead of folding on the road.
lead.whyBullet2: Oklahoma City looked lost, which means their usual strengths completely abandoned them — that's either a fluke or a sign they're cracking under pressure.
lead.whatToSay: Spurs reminded everyone that 27 points in a Finals game is not a playoff basketball team, that's a team that's broken.

markets.mood: Microsoft's 3.5% pop dragged the whole market higher today—broad gains across tech and chips suggest investors got more comfortable with rate expectations, even if it's just a small relief rally.
markets.whyBullet1: Your 401k probably has tech exposure, and when MSFT and NVDA move together like this, it's a real tailwind for growth accounts.
markets.whyBullet2: Watch the jobs report Friday morning—if hiring comes in hot, this rally gets sold off fast because the Fed stays stubborn on rates.
markets.bringUp: MSFT crushed the field today at 3.5% while BTC barely moved at 0.3%—crypto guys like to talk about uncorrelated assets, but when the real money moves, stocks still lead and everything else follows.

golf.headline: Three guys tied atop Charles Schwab Challenge after early rounds.
golf.whyCare1: This is a FedEx Cup event—every win counts toward playoff seeding and that $25 million prize pool.
golf.whyCare2: Ryan Gerard and Andrew Putnam aren't household names, which means the tour's depth is real and the favorites haven't shown up yet.
golf.watchFor: Tom Kim's weekend. He's tied for the lead at a course that rewards young, aggressive players—if he converts, he's a legitimate threat in the postseason.
golf.whatToSay: Gerard and Putnam are running the board right now, but that leaderboard's going to get crowded once the big names wake up.

culture.topic: Two artists bail on Trump's Freedom concerts
culture.whatHappened: Two artists dropped out of the Trump-backed Freedom 250 concert series scheduled for Washington, D.C.
culture.whyItMatters: High-profile cancellations from a political event signal artists are still weighing the cost of association.
culture.whatToSay: Even with backing, getting A-list talent to commit to political events is harder than it looks.

culture.topic: King Arthur manuscript hits auction block
culture.whatHappened: A King Arthur manuscript that's been in private hands for 700 years is going up for sale with a massive price tag.
culture.whyItMatters: Historic literary artifacts only surface rarely, and the asking price will tell you exactly how valuable old stories still are.
culture.whatToSay: Someone's about to pay insane money for a book that's been gathering dust in a closet for centuries.

culture.whatHappened: Eight episodes shot entirely in black and white across Italy.

copy.finalSharpTake: The Spurs just embarrassed Oklahoma City at home—27 points. That's not a Game 6 performance, that's a statement. San Antonio's defense suffocated them, and suddenly this series feels like it belongs in Texas now. Tech stocks are having their day with Microsoft leading the charge, but watch what happens in Game 7. Everything's equal again.

glance.sports: Spurs demolished Thunder 118-91 in Game 6 to force a Game 7 in the West Finals — series tied 3-3.
glance.market: SPY up 0.6%, QQQ +0.8%, MSFT jumped 3.5% on the day.
glance.bestConvo: San Antonio just proved they're not done yet — blew out OKC at home when it mattered most.
glance.watchNext: Game 7 of Spurs-Thunder tomorrow night — winner heads to the Finals.
glance.quickRec: Ryan Gerard leading the Charles Schwab Challenge at -6 if you've got golf on in the background.
